destinations.evidence: The shared crate is the source of fee component formulas and total calculation.
Payload settlement creation imports the shared fee formulas for phase 1 and phase 2 settlement amounts.
Block verification recomputes and compares settlement fees through the same shared formulas.
destinations.snippet:
let fees = calculate_async_fees(&mut db, get_model_registry_address(), job.precompile_address, &precompile_input, Some(&spc_output), &mut model_cache)?;
if settlement.executor_fee != fees.executor_fee || settlement.commitment_fee != fees.commitment_fee || settlement.inclusion_fee != fees.inclusion_fee || settlement.total_amount != fees.total { return Err(...); }

destinations.evidence: Wallet validation uses the exported shared fee helpers instead of independent local constants.

equivalence_argument: The formulas now live in ritual-async-fees and are consumed by payload settlement creation, block verification, and wallet validation. This preserves builder/verifier/wallet fee parity through one shared module instead of duplicated constants.
notes: Mapped to the shared fee crate and its consuming code paths.
rationale: The new crate documents itself as the single source of truth for block building, block verification, and wallet validation, and the primary diff removes duplicated fee constants/helpers from payload and wallet code while importing ritual_async_fees into payload, wallet validation, and block verification. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Payload settlement creation, block verification, and wallet balance validation must calculate async executor, commitment, and inclusion fees through the shared ritual-async-fees formulas rather than maintaining independent local constants.
suggested_falsification_tests: For the same async precompile address, input, and output, compare the fees used by wallet validation, settlement creation, and settlement verification; fail if any path diverges from ritual_async_fees::calculate_async_fees or its exported component formulas.
summary: Async fee formulas are shared through ritual-async-fees across payload, verifier, and wallet paths.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For one async input/output, compute fees through payload settlement preparation, block verification recomputation, and wallet validation helper paths and assert all component formulas agree with ritual_async_fees exports.
title: Async fees use shared formulas
